items.title: No Potable Water in Park Campgrounds
items.content: There currently is no water available in any campground in Great Basin National Park.  Water is available at the Lehman Caves Visitor Center and some local businesses.

items.title: Inclement Weather Alert
items.content: Thunderstorms and heavy rain may impact visits to Salt River Bay National Historical Park and Ecological Preserve. Flooding is possible in low-lying areas. Trails may become muddy, slippery, and subject to mudslides. Water conditions may also be rough. Some areas or programs may be limited or closed. Use caution and follow staff guidance. Check https://www.weather.gov/sju/ for current conditions.

items.title: Inclement Weather Alert
items.content: High winds, rough seas, and heavy rain may impact visits to Buck Island Reef National Monument. Hazardous conditions can affect boat travel, snorkeling, and beach access. Flooding rains may cause muddy, slippery trails and possible mudslides. Trips may be delayed or canceled. Use caution and follow guidance. Check https://www.weather.gov/sju/ for current conditions.

items.title: Inclement Weather Alert
items.content: Thunderstorms and localized flooding may impact Christiansted NHS. Low-lying and waterfront areas may flood, and surfaces may be slippery. Weather conditions may impact your travel plans. Use caution and follow staff guidance. Check current conditions at https://www.weather.gov/sju/ before and during your visit.

items.title: Johnson's Reef Buoys are OFF STATION
items.content: The aids to navigation around Johnson's Reef are currently off station. This means that the buoys that typically guide mariners through these waters are not in their expected locations. Mariners should exercise considerable caution when navigating the waters around Johnson's Reef. The absence or misplacement of navigational aids can lead to confusion, increased risk of grounding, and potential hazards. Use updated nautical charts and GPS systems to navigate carefully through the area.

items.title: Flood Advisory issued April 13 at 1:13PM AST until April 13 at 3:15PM AST b
items.content: Cruz Bay Visitor Center and Trunk Bay Fee Station are closed as of 12:30PM 04/13/2026. Visitors should exercise extreme caution when out on roads, trails and beaches. It is advisable to find alternative activities until the weather forecast changes.

items.title: Averill–Kelly Creek Wilderness Segment — Dangerous Ford at New Wood River
items.content: The New Wood River has risen and may be too high to safely ford. To bypass this section from the west, follow CTH‑E north for 1.9 miles, then continue east for 3.0 miles. At Burma Road, head south for 1.0 mile to rejoin the Ice Age Trail. Stay alert. Use caution.

items.title: 4/13/26 Fire ban in effect
items.content: Due to high fire danger and dry conditions, Shenandoah National Park has issued a parkwide fire ban which prohibits all open-air fires. Only gas and or propane stoves are permitted at this time.

items.title: Skunk and Foster Lakes Segment — Storm Damage
items.content: Storm damage west of North Foley Road has left many downed trees, and the white‑blazed loop may be impassable. Use caution if hiking this area, or use North Foley Road to bypass it. Volunteers are assessing conditions and working to clear the Trail.

items.title: NPS 300 Road Conditions
items.content: NPS 300 to Big Tubes is dry and has been recently graded. It is currently passable for most vehicles.

items.title: Hemlock Creek Segment — Logging Activities
items.content: Logging operations along the Hemlock Creek Segment may mean heavy machinery and trucks in the area. The Ice Age Trail follows a logging road used for storage and transport of logs. The Trail is open. Stay alert. Remain on the trail. Use caution. Keep dogs leashed.

items.title: Indian Creek Segment — Machinery and Parking Closure
items.content: Recent logging near CTH-E (15th Street) may mean heavy machinery and log piles in the area. The parking lot on CTH-E remains closed to hikers. The Ice Age Trail is open. Stay alert. Remain on the trail. Use caution. Keep dogs leashed.

items.title: Protect Yourself from Volcanic Tephra
items.content: Volcanic ash, rock and glassy strands (tephra) is present at Kīlauea summit and can irritate your skin, eyes and respiratory system. For your safety, do not handle it. Eye protection, long sleeves, long pants, a hat, closed-toe shoes and a dust mask are recommended.

items.title: Kīlauea eruption
items.content: Volcanic eruptions can be hazardous. Stay out of closed areas and monitor air quality. Eruptions can last a few hours and are often separated by pauses lasting several weeks.

items.title: Great Allegheny Passage Erosion Near Confluence, Pennsylvania (Mile Marker 56)
items.content: Heavy rain & flash floods on June 8 eroded a section of trail along the Great Allegheny Passage between Rockwood and Confluence. Crews from Somerset County have placed caution tape for awareness. The GAP remains open at that location; please be cautious.

items.title: Greenbush Segment — Logging Activities
items.content: A timber harvest is taking place on the Greenbush Segment. The work affects about 0.1 mile of Trail between Shelter #5 and the Greenbush Picnic Area. Heavy machinery may be in the area. The Ice Age Trail is open. Stay alert. Remain on the trail. Use caution. Keep dogs leashed.

items.title: Tisch Mills Segment — Seasonal Flooding
items.content: The Ice Age Trail from Mill Road to Nuclear Road is often flooded in spring or after heavy rain. To bypass this section, follow CTH-B between CTH-BB and Nuclear Road.

items.title: Hilux Wildfire
items.content: The Hilux Wildfire, discovered on April 3, continues to burn in the northwest corner of the preserve, north of I-75. Due to the fire progression, some areas have been closed for use, including Bear Island, Gator Head, and Pink Jeep Campgrounds, as well as trails in the area. Visitors should use caution and remain clear of all areas north of I-75 and west of Bundschu Grade. For area closure map click more.

items.title: Road Construction Near the Park
items.content: There is road construction near the park visitor center along 111th St. and S. Cottage Avenue. Please use caution and allow extra travel time when visiting. Construction is expected to last through the fall. In addition, on occasion the vehicle entrance for parking at 610 E. 111th street is limited to one lane.

items.title: Barataria Visitor Center and Trails are Closed
items.content: Because of upcoming demolition and construction of the trails and Visitor Center, all trails at the Barataria Preserve and the Visitor Center are closed, with the exception of Twin Canals Trail This is a day-use area only with no restrooms. Please join us Wednesdays and Thursdays 10:00 am - 2:00 pm for programming and guided hikes along the Wetland Trace trail at 1:00 pm at the temporary Town Hall (at the Lafitte Barataria Museum location) and Wetland Trace, 4917 City Park Drive, Jean Lafitte, 70067.

items.title: High Fire Danger and Fire Ban
items.content: Due to high fire danger throughout the park, a fire ban has been put into place. As a result, the lighting of campfires is not allowed at this time. Charcoal grills are permissible as long as the charcoal is attended during use.

items.title: Blue Basin Overlook Trail Closed
items.content: The 3.25 - mile Blue Basin Overlook trail in the Sheep Rock unit is closed until further notice. A mudslide and trail washouts have created unsafe conditions for hiking. The shorter 1.3- mile Island In Time trail remains open.

items.title: Conditions and Construction Affecting Cataract Canyon Takeouts
items.content: The primitive, dirt take-out at North Wash is undergoing construction this spring which might cause delays of up to 5 hours. Rafters are advised to use the foot trail past Highway 95 for passenger & gear drop-off (steep incline), continue on to Bullfrog, or plan their take-out when construction is not active: (Friday–Sunday or before 7am / after 6pm Monday–Thursday).

items.title: Conditions and Construction Affecting Cataract Canyon Takeouts
items.content: Hite Ramp is closed. The primitive, dirt take-out at North Wash is undergoing construction this spring which might cause delays of up to 5 hours. Boaters are advised to use the foot trail past Highway 95 for passenger & gear drop-off (steep incline), continue on to Bullfrog, or plan their take-out when construction is not active: (Friday–Sunday or before 7am / after 6pm Monday–Thursday).

items.title: Construction Delays
items.content: There are heavy traffic delays in both directions before entering the park., Please use caution.

items.title: Temporary Emergency Fire Restrictions
items.content: Due to dry conditions and high fire danger, open fires are prohibited in Wind Cave National Park until further notice. Gas stoves used in or on fire resistant containers are permitted only in the Elk Mountain Campground and the Picnic Area. No smoking is allowed except in vehicles or on paved or concrete areas next to the visitor center. Fires of any kind on trails or in backcountry areas are prohibited. This includes smoking, gas stoves and open fires.

items.title: Beat the Heat!
items.content: Make sure to take weather into consideration when visiting. Carry water and use sunscreen to reduce the risk of heat injuries during your visit.

items.title: Photo Season
items.content: Just a reminder as the weather is starting to get nice, and people are thinking about where to take graduation, wedding, prom or formal, pregnancy, birthday, and any other type of photography or filming! We do have rules and regulations on what is or is not allowed and whether or not you need a permit. Violations may be ticketed. Please check our website under Plan Your Visit, Permits and Reservations!!! https://www.nps.gov/frhi/planyourvisit/permits.htm

items.title: Stage 1 Burn Restrictions
items.content: Discharge of fireworks are prohibited. Only campfires at campsites within a fire ring are permitted. Charcoal fires within enclosed grills are permitted. Portable stoves are permitted.

items.title: Oasis of Mara Trail Partial Closure
items.content: Due to a heavy flooding event, the Oasis of Mara Trail is closed past the Oasis.

items.title: Storm Damage
items.content: Some of our trails are open but most of our eastside trails are inaccessible. Please use caution throughout the site especially when hiking, broken tree parts continue to fall.

items.title: Blasting in Memorial
items.content: Blasting activity is occurring in the Coronado National Forest, Huachuca Mountains, and within Coronado National Memorial. Visitors may hear loud noise and notice dust in the air.

items.title: Spring 2026 Prescribed Fire Notice at Lake Roosevelt
items.content: Lake Roosevelt National Recreation Area may implement the following prescribed burn during the spring of 2026.

items.title: Scam Warning - Book ferry tickets through concessioner site only
items.content: Valid ferry tickets can only be purchased through the website www.cumberlandislandferry.com. Check site carefully before purchasing. Only valid tickets will be honored.

items.title: Avian Influenza (HPAI) Suspected in Dead Birds in Seashore Areas
items.content: Dead birds observed along beaches and marsh shorelines may have been infected with Highly Pathogenic Avian Influenza (HPAI). The New York State Department of Environmental Conservation (NYSDEC) advises the public to avoid contact with sick or dead birds and mammals that may be infected with HPAI. Pets should be secured at all times and kept away from all wild animals. See the link below for more information.

items.title: A harmful algal bloom (HAB) has been detected in Government Wash
items.content: Bright green algae (cyanobacteria) floating on the water may contain toxins and is unsafe for swimming or contact. Children and pets are at higher risk if contaminated water is swallowed. Keep pets leashed and away from the water. Seek medical or veterinary care if symptoms occur after exposure. Click More for photos of the harmful algae as well as symptoms in humans or pets if exposed to the bacteria.

items.title: BE AWARE OF HIGH-WATER DANGER
items.content: Visitors should be aware of high-water danger. Heavy rain has made crossing flowing water hazardous for hikers and horseback riders.

items.title: Elevated Fire Risk
items.content: Fire danger is especially high in southeastern Arizona March through June. Red Flag Warnings and fire restrictions may be implemented without notice. Campers: be prepared to go without a campfire and to cook using an enclosed propane device. Be cautious at all times and use water to drown fires. Note: Ground fires and wood collection are prohibited at all times. Stand up grills are available only for overnight campers.

items.title: Bacteria Levels at Arizona Hot Springs
items.content: Bacterial levels in water samples taken from Arizona Hot Springs exceed Local, State and National Park Service standards for recreational activity. Due to the increased human health risk, the National Park Service warns to avoid contact with these waters until further notice. Please contact the Park at 702-293-8990 for updates on this advisory.

items.title: High Levels of Visitation
items.content: The park will be experiencing high levels of visitation from March through May. Expect limited parking, long lines, and crowded areas. Please have a credit or debit card ready or valid park pass with ID when checking in at the visitor center.

items.title: High Levels of Visitation
items.content: The park will be experiencing high levels of visitation from March through May. Expect limited parking, long lines, and crowded areas. Please have a credit or debit card ready or valid park pass with ID when checking in at the visitor center. Parking or stopping is not permitted on the roads outside the park gate at any time.

items.title: DC Water Sewer Overflow
items.content: DC Water is managing a sanitary sewer overflow from the Potomac Interceptor near Lock 10. Anyone with questions or in need of support can reach out to DC Water’s 24-Hour Command Center at (202) 612-3400 or visit https://www.dcwater.com/about-dc-water/media/potomac-interceptor-collapse.

items.title: All Trails on Shiloh Battlefield Closed
items.content: Due to tree and limb hazards from the recent ice storm, all trails on the battlefield are considered closed. Cleanup operations have begun on the park. Please be aware of workers and equipment on the roadways.

items.title: St. Croix Falls Segment — Construction & Intermittent Closures
items.content: Construction activity near the St. Croix Falls school complex may mean machinery in the area. The Ice Age Trail remains open. Trail closures and detours are anticipated in the spring. To bypass when hiking eastbound, head north on Jackson Street, east on Louisiana Street, and south on Blanding Woods Road.

items.title: 2-hour delayed opening 20, February 2026 due to inclement weather.
items.content: Sunset Crater Volcano National Monument will have a 2-hour delayed opening on 20, February 2026 due to inclement weather and area icy road conditions. The monument will open at 11am.

items.title: 2-hour delayed opening 20, February 2026 due to inclement weather.
items.content: Walnut Canyon National Monument will have a 2-hour delayed opening on 20, February 2026 due to inclement weather and area icy road conditions . The monument will open at 11am.

items.title: Road workers on Park Road in Davis Bayou
items.content: Phase II of the Davis Bayou Bicycle and Pedestrian Path is in process. Visitors may encounter minor traffic delays and flaggers on Eagle Point and Park Road.

items.title: Speed Limits are Much Lower Inside Park than Surrounding Highways
items.content: Speed limits for the park include 30 MPH near the park boundary and Great Sand Dunes Sign, and as low as 10 MPH near the Headquarters and Entrance Station. Speed limits are in place to protect park visitors, pedestrians, and wildlife in the park. Please drive carefully and responsibly.

items.title: State Route 148 Closed Through Cedar Breaks National Monument
items.content: State Route 148 through Cedar Breaks National Monument is now closed for its routine winter season. Plan alternate routes and check back for updates. Visit the Utah Department of Transportation website for more route information: www.udottraffic.utah.gov

items.title: Roadwork Along Lakeshore Drive at the Alfred Smith Water Treatment Plant
items.content: Starting February 9th, 2026, roadwork will begin on the east shoulder of Lakeshore Drive between the water treatment plant and the Lake Mead Fish Hatchery for the installation of a pipeline. Flaggers will be present during the project, but only minimal traffic delays are anticipated at times that traffic is restricted to one lane. The project is expected to be completed in a few months.

items.title: Vehicle Weight Limit at Kenilworth Aquatic Gardens Entrance
items.content: Due to structural conditions at the driveway entrance, access to the Kenilworth Aquatic Gardens main entrance and parking lot is limited to vehicles with a gross vehicle weight rating (GVWR) of 16,000 pounds or less.

items.title: Construction at Muir Home Site
items.content: Construction is ongoing at the home site and the surrounding areas. Disruption to normal visitation may occur.

items.title: Life Jackets Required!
items.content: By Nebraska State law, all children 13 years old and younger must WEAR a USCG approved life jacket to float the Niobrara NSR. Anyone over 13 years old must HAVE a life jacket. If you are renting equipment, your outfitter will provide them.

items.title: Pavement Project- Playalinda District
items.content: The Playalinda Beach District continues a Federal Highways pavement preservation project that started on Sept. 22nd, 2025. Visitors to the seashore could expect limited access to some parking areas and roadways to include the vistas.

items.title: Hopeton Earthworks Closed for Construction Until May 2026
items.content: Hopeton Earthworks, located at 990 Hopetown Rd. Chillicothe, OH 45601, is closed for construction until May 2026. Parking lot improvements and construction activity will block safe access to cars and foot traffic at the trail heads. Call the park at 740-774-1126 for more information or visit the park's Mound City Group Visitor Center at 16062 State Route 104, Chillicothe, OH 45601.

items.title: Winter Hazards on Trails - Downed Trees and High Water
items.content: While park staff clear trails several times each year, please be prepared for downed trees. Rain and wind continue to bring down dead trees within the Carr megafire burn scar, and this trend will continue for years. Also note high water at creek fords and seasonal streams during this time of year. Enjoy your national recreation area but please be safe out there.

items.title: Construction work at entrance to Visitor Center parking lot
items.content: Construction work at the entrance to the Visitor Center parking lot will cause single-lane road closures from January through March. Please use caution and follow signage or directions from workers.

items.title: Water is Required
items.content: Due to the trail being steep of 12 percent grade, all visitors wishing to hike the Lower Cliff Dwelling Trail must bring a water bottle with them. No Exceptions.

items.title: Park Wide Caution
items.content: Visitors should exercise caution when hiking areas of the park. Hazards on trails and in forested areas such as hanging tree limbs, debris on the ground, and washed-out sections of trails and bridges are possible.

items.title: Park Wide Caution
items.content: Visitors should exercise caution when hiking areas of the park that have opened since Hurricane Helene. Hazards on trails and in forested areas such as hanging tree limbs, debris on the ground, and washed-out sections of trails and bridges are possible.

items.title: Acadian Cultural Center Temporarily Closed
items.content: The Acadian Cultural Center in Lafayette is temporarily closed due to HVAC issues. Join us at the Prairie Acadian Cultural Center in Eunice for programs and events 5 days a week, Tuesday through Saturday.

items.title: Capacity Restrictions at the Reconstructed Fort
items.content: The design of the reconstructed fort fails to meet accessibility, fire, and life-safety requirements of 1976. A lack of code compliant components such as emergency lighting, illuminated exit signage, panic hardware on doors, and outwardly swinging exit doors results in a limitation of 49 occupants for visitor operations within the reconstructed fort.

items.title: Plaza Access to Reconstructed Fort
items.content: Entry into the reconstructed fort is limited to the central plaza on scheduled days. For your safety respect all closures and restrictions. Guided tours provide access into additional areas.

items.title: 8 Miles of Unpaved Road to Reach Park
items.content: In order to reach the Sand Creek Massacre Site, visitors must travel on at least 8 miles of unpaved roads. We are not able to guarantee road conditions of county roads 54 or W after spring storms.

items.title: Parking at Boundary Access Areas
items.content: Visitors are reminded to park in designated parking areas when accessing the park via boundary trailheads. Parking in unauthorized locations may result in your vehicle being towed at the owner's expense. Park on the right side of the road approaching Thompson Hollow. Parking is authorized up to the "No Parking" sign before a private driveway.

items.title: Backcountry Trail Conditions
items.content: GPS is necessary on backcountry trails: Oakridge, Boggy Gut (formerly River), and Kingsnake. These trails may be difficult to follow.

items.title: Park Hours may Change due to Winter Storms
items.content: Opening delays or temporary closures due to blowing snow, accumulation, and icy roads are likely when these conditions exist. Roads will reopen when snowplows have cleared them for safe two-way travel. Before visiting, check this page for up-to-date information. Colorado Traction Law may apply, see info below. Click link for weather.

items.title: No Gasoline Available in Park
items.content: The gas station at Mazama Village has closed for the season. No gasoline is available for purchase in the park. Running out of gas will require calling a tow truck to assist. Please plan accordingly!

items.title: Be City Smart: Take precautions to secure your vehicle and valuables.
items.content: This is an urban park. Don't hide valuables inside your car. Carry items with you on hike. Lock your vehicle and set car alarm. Report suspicious persons loitering/peeking in cars. Call 928-638-7805

items.title: Use caution on Cima Road
items.content: Use caution on Cima Road due to shoulder washouts.

items.title: Rough and damaged roads
items.content: Drive slowly and exercise due caution when driving in Mojave National Preserve. Rough and damaged roads exist on both paved and unpaved roads. Watch for potholes.

items.title: Trails Not Maintained During Winter
items.content: Trails at River Raisin National Battlefield Park are not maintained during winter months. Please use caution.

items.title: Limited Amphitheater Accessibility
items.content: Construction vehicles are operating through the parking lot & Lower Overlook Park - use caution & be vigilant for hazards. Lower Overlook & Amphitheater access is via stairs only for construction duration.

items.title: Tickets are required to enter Timpanogos Cave
items.content: Reservations are strongly recommended. Reservations are the only way to ensure a spot is available for your party. Please plan accordingly by visiting Recreation.gov to secure your tickets in advance. Availability of walk-up tickets is not guaranteed.

items.title: Use Caution with "Fossil Beds Information" Signs
items.content: The new visitor center is located at 17970 US Hwy 30, about one mile north of downtown Hagerman and the old visitor center. Highway signs are not up-to-date.

items.title: Lakina River Update
items.content: A recently observed landslide across the Lakina River is creating a debris hazard on a popular packrafting route. The river water is cutting its way through, but is highly dynamic and should be expected to remain that way through the boating season.

items.title: Auto-Theft Warning - Only Bring What You Need to the Park
items.content: You can prevent vehicle break-ins in parking lots and scenic overlooks. Before leaving your car for even a short period of time: 1) Remove all valuables, 2) Lock your doors. 3) Take your cell phones and electronics with you. Read article for more details.

items.title: Water Outage at Ponderosa Campground
items.content: The Ponderosa Campground is experiencing a temporary water outage. Please bring all the water you’ll need for your visit, including drinking water.

items.title: Possible Bridge Closure
items.content: The Circle Trail bridge under Winnewissa Falls may be closed temporarily due to flooding caused by spring rain. Please pay attention to signage posted in the Visitor Center and at the bridge. The rest of the park and Circle Trail remain open.

items.title: Stehekin Flash Flood and Debris Flow Area
items.content: The Stehekin area is prone to flooding, which increases in a post-fire environment. Know before you go – visit the Post Fire Safety page for information on what to do in a flash flood and debris flow area

items.title: If You Can't Reach Us By Phone, Please Email Us
items.content: Please call 208-933-4105 to talk to a park ranger. However, if the phone lines are down, contact a ranger through email at hafo_information@nps.gov

items.title: Construction
items.content: There is ongoing construction due to roofing project. Please practice caution on the grounds and do not enter marked areas.

items.title: Roads May Be Icy - Please Use Caution!
items.content: Even though the sun is shining, the open roads in the park may still have icy patches, especially in the mornings and evenings when the temperatures drop. Please drive with caution.

items.title: Canyon from Toe of Exit Glacier to the Outwash Plain
items.content: The canyon from the toe of Exit Glacier to where Exit Creek opens into the Outwash Plain is susceptible to sudden outburst flood events. These events produce sudden surges of water, ice and rock. For your safety please stick to designated park trails.

items.title: River Trail periodically closed under I-16
items.content: The section of the River Trail beneath Interstate 16 is closed during bridge construction when pedestrian safety is at risk and remains open when no significant hazards are present.

items.title: Ongoing work on Lower Town buildings
items.content: Facilities staff are doing repair work on several buildings in Lower Town. Please follow signs for alternate entrances and heed temporary closures.

items.title: Glover Archbold Trail detour
items.content: For visitor safety, the southern end of the Glover Archbold Trail is under an emergency closure until further notice due to aerial hazards. Please follow the posted detour along Foxhall Rd. / 44th St. NW / P St. NW.

items.title: Hazardous Conditions on Trails
items.content: Visitors should exercise caution when hiking areas of the park that have opened since Hurricane Helene. Hazards on trails and in forested areas such as hanging tree limbs, debris on the ground, and washed-out sections of trails and bridges are possible.

items.title: Feral Dogs May Be Seen in Organ Pipe Cactus National Monument
items.content: Feral dogs may be present at the Twin Peaks Campground and around the Kris Eggle Visitor Center. Visitors should not attempt to feed or water these dogs or approach them in any way as they may carry disease or become hostile.

items.title: Most Monument Roads Require Heavy Duty 4WD Vehicle to Travel Safely
items.content: Breaking down in the remote monument can be a life-threatening emergency. Most roads require a well-equipped, heavy-duty 4WD vehicle to travel. 2WD high clearance vehicles must use Walking Box Ranch Rd for entry and exit. No Visitor Facilities Available.

items.title: Ongoing Closure Notices
items.content: Devils Island dock and campsites, West Bay Club on Sand Island, and the trail on Stockton Island between Presque Isle Bay and Quarry Bay are closed. These closures affect only a small portion of the Lakeshore. Enjoy your visit.

items.title: Rip Currents and Rough Surf
items.content: Remember that rough surf is dangerous & rip currents can be deadly. Please use caution when swimming at Canaveral National Seashore.

items.title: Trail Conditions
items.content: Rain may cause trails to be slick and overgrown. Staff and Volunteer Trail Crews clear trails regularly. Visitors should use caution and report trail issues to viis_interpretation@nps.gov

items.title: Help Prevent Aquatic Invasive Species (AIS) Infestation
items.content: A number of lakes are infested with an array of aquatic invasive species that damage natural ecosystems and waterways. Boaters are advised to Clean, Drain, and Dry all boats and equipment every time they leave the water.

items.title: Black Bears Frequenting the McAfee Knob Area in Virginia
items.content: Human-habituated bears have been frequently spotted in the McAfee Knob Area. Allowing bears access to human food or garbage causes it to lose its fear of humans. Follow all food storage regulations, use provided food lockers and never feed a bear.

items.title: Lasting Effects from 2021 Dixie Fire
items.content: Some facilities, infrastructure, and areas remain closed; please adhere to all closure signs. In re-opened areas, hazards like fallen trees, loose/falling rock, undefined trails, and hidden stump holes exist. Click more to learn about burned area safety.

items.title: Use Caution in and around Pedersen Lagoon
items.content: On August 7, 2024, a landslide triggered tsunami washed over portions of Pedersen Lagoon near Aialik Bay (59.8842N, -149.7309W). We recommend caution for those visiting the area in and around Pedersen Lagoon. Click more for additional information.

items.title: New Food Storage Guidelines to Reduce Human - Wildlife Interactions
items.content: In response to wolves accessing human food and garbage in and around Rock Harbor and campgrounds located on the east end of the island, new food storage guidelines to ensure the safety of visitors and wildlife are in effect.

items.title: Canine distemper is believed to be present in the park.
items.content: Contact with saliva from wild animals can transmit distemper to pets, including from but not limited to water bowls, food bowls, trash etc. Distemper is not a threat to humans but can be deadly to pets. Ensure your pets are vaccinated or keep them home.

items.title: Sick Birds and Wildlife, H5N1
items.content: Be cautious around sea lions, and dead or sick birds. If you get “bombed” by a bird, wash with soap and water immediately, monitor for signs of illness, and call your doctor. Report beached, sick or distressed wildlife to Wildlife Mortality Reporting.

items.title: Bear Alert
items.content: Black bears inhabit many of the Apostle Islands. Follow safety guidelines: store food and scented items properly, hike in groups, stay attentive, and make noises if you encounter a bear in a visitor area.

items.title: Overlook Park Parking Lot - Vehicles Must Be Parked in a Designated Spot
items.content: Parking is extremely limited on weekends & event days. Overflow parking is available at the Paterson Museum, located at 2 Market Street. On weekends only, additional parking is available at the lot across from the Museum.

items.title: Hiking in Gold Mine Tract
items.content: Visitors are restricted to the designated trails within the Gold Mine Tract in the Great Falls area. This location is generally defined as that area bordered by MacArthur Boulevard and Berma Road and depicted on the Great Falls Maryland Hiking Map.

items.title: Inaccurate Third-Party Apps
items.content: Some third-party hiking apps are providing inaccurate trail and safety information to users. Please use the official NPS app for accurate information.

items.title: River Ferry at Akers (on State Highway K) CLOSED until further notice
items.content: The Akers Ferry on State Highway K will not be in service until further notice.

items.title: Lower Crypt Access Subject to Safe Conditions
items.content: Access to the lower crypt area of the Mausoleum is subject to closure during wet and or low light conditions in the late afternoon. The main floor and the two reliquary rooms remain open during these conditions.

items.title: The Use of GPS is NOT Recommended
items.content: Numerous visitors have been routed to remote trailheads and out-of-the-way roads trying to use GPS to reach the main park area or shuttle stop.

items.title: Pozo Nuevo Road to Bates Well Ranch High Clearance, 4-Wheel Drive Only
items.content: High Clearance, 4-wheel drive is required from where Pozo Nuevo Road begins along South Puerto Blanch Drive to Bates Well Ranch area. The road is washed out in areas. Bates Well Ranch is still accessible from the north on Darby Well Road.

items.title: Vehicle Break-in and Theft Warning
items.content: Car break-ins in Cuyahoga Valley have tripled since 2022, resulting in theft of personal items like credit cards. Do not leave valuables in your car. As a last resort, lock items in your trunk before arriving. Thieves target parking lots regularly.

items.title: Auto-Theft Warning - Only Bring What You Need to the Park
items.content: You can help prevent vehicle break-ins in parking lots. Before leaving your car to catch the ferry: 1) Remove all valuables, 2) Lock your doors. 3) Take your cell phones and electronics with you. Read article for more details.

items.title: Auto-Theft Warning - Only Bring What You Need to the Park
items.content: You can prevent vehicle break-ins in parking lots and scenic overlooks. Before leaving your car for even a short period of time: 1) Remove all valuables, 2) Lock your doors. 3) Take your cell phones and electronics with you. Read article for more details.

items.title: Auto-Theft Warning - Only Bring What You Need to the Park
items.content: You can prevent vehicle break-ins in parking lots and scenic overlooks. Before leaving your car for even a short period of time: 1) Remove all valuables, 2) Lock your doors. 3) Take your cell phones and electronics with you. Read article for more details.

items.title: Wildlife Viewing Guidelines
items.content: San Juan Island National Historical Park provides habitat for many species of animals. When you visit the park, you are visiting their home. Do not approach any wildlife within 75 ft/23 m (two school bus lengths).

items.title: Roadwork may cause delays on US 41/Tamiami Trail
items.content: Current roadwork is ongoing along the park's northern boundary on US Highway 41/Tamiami Trail to raise the road for Everglades Restoration benefits. Drivers may experience up to 15-minute delays and should watch for personnel, equipment and slow downs.

items.title: Passaic River Trail Bridge washed out
items.content: Tropical Storm Ida washed out the Passaic River bridge on the Patriot's Path Trail south of Tempe Wick Road. This area is a known habitat of the endangered wood turtle, so do not wade, walk, or let your dog run in the river at this location.

items.title: Diablo Lake Boat Launch Inaccessible to Powerboats
items.content: Due to silt accumulation, the Diablo Lake boat launch at Colonial Creek South Campground is not usable for launching most powerboats. Expect shallow or no water at the launch ramp and the possibility of needing to carry your boat to or from the water.

items.title: SEASONAL SAFETY MESSAGE
items.content: Be careful and safe while out in the canyon! Be cautious of your surroundings, don't cliff jump, don't stray off of the main trails, and don't get too close to Little River Falls or the canyon rim. These activities could result in injury or death.

items.title: Toxic Cyanobacteria in the Virgin River and the streams of Zion National Park
items.content: Do not submerge your head in or filter drinking water from still or moving water.

items.title: Avoid area around the church & under trees in high wind conditions
items.content: In high wind conditions (sustained winds of 25 mph and above) visitors are advised to avoid the area around the church, with the possibility of falling clay tiles from the roof, and the cemetery, possibility of falling tree branches or limbs. Thank you.

items.title: Weather Advisory and Storm Warnings
items.content: The park may have delayed openings or closures in the event of severe weather. Travel may not be recommended during these times. Please check local weather forecasts. We appreciate your understanding.

items.title: Weather Advisory and Storm Warnings
items.content: The park may have delayed openings or closures in the event of severe weather. Travel may not be recommended during these times. Please check local weather forecasts. We appreciate your understanding.

items.title: Weather Advisory and Storm Warnings
items.content: The park may have delayed openings or closures in the event of severe weather. Travel may not be recommended during these times. Please check local forecasts. We appreciate your understanding.

items.title: Be Aware of Illegal Traffic
items.content: Smuggling and/or illegal entry is common in this area due to the proximity of the international border. Please be aware of your surroundings at all times and do not travel alone in remote areas.

items.title: Devil's Hall is a Strenuous Hike
items.content: The hike to Devil's Hall is rated as strenuous. The portion of the route in the wash requires rock scrambling and is dangerous when wet or if water is present. The wash surface is loose gravel and rocks and may be unstable.

items.title: Boater Awareness
items.content: Please use caution in park waters due to navigational markers that may be missing or moved due to unforeseen circumstances.

items.title: Dangerous Highway Crossing
items.content: Highway 177 is 60 mph with a blind spot directly across from the house. Safely use the highway underpass to the Fox Creek Trail just south of the visitor center parking lot. Be smart, stay alive.

items.title: Rip Current Status
items.content: Review rip current status prior to visiting the park.

items.title: Navigation
items.content: Do not rely on GPS digital navigation systems to find your way to Badlands National Park. Please note that the Visitor Center at 25216 Ben Reifel Road, Interior SD 57750 is located just off I-90.

items.title: Notice to travelers to National Cemetery
items.content: Due to the narrow roadway & tight turn, RVs, mobile homes, charter buses, delivery trucks, or vehicles with trailers are prohibited from the crest of Monument Hill. Check for information about parking at the Visitor Center upon your arrival.

items.title: Garden Key Harbor Light
items.content: Vessel operators - the Garden Key Harbor Light is undergoing repairs and is extinguished. At this time it is unknown when the light will again be illuminated.

items.title: Warning about GPS Systems!
items.content: Vehicle Navigation Systems and GPS units may provide inaccurate information—sending drivers the wrong way on one-way roads, leading them to dead ends in remote areas, or sending them on roads which are impassable. Use the Interstate exits for wayfinding.

items.title: Test Alert
items.content: Test Alert

items.title: Navigation
items.content: Please do not rely upon GPS digital navigation systems to find your way to Craters of the Moon. Please note that the Visitor Center at 1266 Craters Loop Road is located just off U.S. Highway 20/26/93 and is not accessed via any unpaved roads.

items.title: Be Aware of Break-ins along State Route 20
items.content: Prevent smashed windows and theft. Break-ins are not uncommon at trailheads along State Route 20. Remove your valuables and electronics and take associated electronic cords.

items.title: Kiwis urged to get naturing this Conservation Week
items.content: Conservation Week/ Te Wiki o Te Taiao kicks off on April 20, with hundreds of events happening across the country and a simple call to all New Zealanders: get naturing.

items.title: New bridge underway for Tongariro River Trail
items.content: Progress is coming to the Tongariro River Trail, with construction of a new suspension bridge - right next to the existing Major Jones bridge - due to begin on Monday 20 April.

items.title: Census shows jump in the population of our rarest endemic bird
items.content: If you’re out naturing on Northland or Auckland’s beaches, spotting a tara iti (NZ fairy tern) is like finding a needle in a haystack.

items.title: Spectacular new Hooker Valley bridge to open later this year
items.content: The massive new suspension bridge being built up the Hooker Valley at Aoraki/Mount Cook will open in late July/early August this year.

items.title: Orca incident prompts call for information
items.content: Enjoy the wonder of orca but please keep your distance.

items.title: Hunters on target at police, DOC forest checkpoint
items.content: DOC and NZ Police say they’re encouraged by the sensible behaviour, positive response and correct paperwork they encountered during a pre-Easter hunting compliance operation.

items.title: Watch for wild dogs in Waioweka
items.content: DOC is calling for hunters to report sightings of a pack of feral dogs spotted recently in Waioweka Conservation Area.

items.title: DOC statement: Outcome of Wildlife Act investigation
items.content: Read DOC's statement re: Outcome of Wildlife Act investigation focused on stony corals.

items.title: Rat detections on predator free island highlights importance of biosecurity network
items.content: A second rat incursion on predator free Ulva Island this year highlights the value of the island's strong biosecurity surveillance network.

items.title: Historic viaducts closed for safety, Hump Ridge Track remains open
items.content: People out naturing in Southern Fiordland this long weekend should be aware of the ongoing closure of three historic structures.

items.title: Clean gear to stop weed spread on Easter outings
items.content: Those heading out naturing over Easter are asked to check and clean their gear before getting into the hills, to stop the spread of invasive weeds.

items.title: Kapiti takes aim at 'possums of freshwater'
items.content: A Kapiti Coast catchment is well on the way to becoming free of koi carp, a noxious introduced fish that leaves a trail of destruction.

items.title: DOC hut with ‘best view in New Zealand’ gets a makeover
items.content: The iconic Mueller Hut in the Aoraki/Mount Cook National Park has had a spruce up, which is no easy feat.

items.title: New Zealand leads protection of world’s rarest seabirds
items.content: Some of the world’s rarest seabirds have a new level of international protection following adoption of a New Zealand-led proposal under the Convention on Migratory Species.

items.title: Kiwi caught on camera a Pureora milestone
items.content: A rare kiwi has been captured on a trail camera in the northern Pureora Forest's Waipapa block – the first recorded sighting of the species in the area for more than 25 years.

items.title: DOC investigates black-billed gull deaths
items.content: DOC is appealing for information on the poisoning of threatened and protected native gulls on the Te Anau lakefront this summer.

items.title: AI traps for Heaphy a first for DOC
items.content: An AI trap network being set up around huts on the Heaphy Track will reduce both pest numbers and time spent maintaining traps on this iconic New Zealand walk.

items.title: Illegal fishing spikes in marine reserve, public tips welcomed
items.content: DOC staff say illegal fishing activity this summer in Te Pātaka o Rākaihautū/Banks Peninsula’s Pōhatu marine reserve is the highest in 10 years.

items.title: Ready, set, naturing: DOC Great Walk, hut and campsite bookings open from 12 May
items.content: Dust off the tramping boots and warm up those clicking fingers - bookings for DOC's Great Walks, huts and campsites for the 2026/27 season open from 12 May.

items.title: Hunters asked to watch for weeds this roar
items.content: Hunters operating in New Zealand's remote places during the roar are being asked to keep an eye out for invasive weeds and report any sightings, and to clean their gear before heading out.

items.title: Wellington Te Whanganui-a-Tara to lead next phase of Predator Free 2050
items.content: Wellington Te Whanganui-a-Tara is set to become New Zealand’s first predator-free city, as the Government steps up the next phase of Predator Free 2050, Conservation Minister Tama Potaka says.

items.title: Endangered kakī/black stilts gobble up hearts
items.content: One of the world’s rarest wading birds, the kakī/black stilt, is feeling the love from one of New Zealand’s largest red meat processors, Alliance Group.

items.title: Competitive allocation process applications closed for NZ's largest farm
items.content: The application period for potential operators wanting to run commercial farming in Molesworth Recreation Reserve has closed.

items.title: Mangakara Nature Walk to reopen, caution urged
items.content: The popular Mangakara Nature Walk in Pirongia Forest Park has reopened today as DOC continues clean-up, repair and assessment work after the wild storm that swept across Waikato in February.

items.title: Last chance to save globally rare plants from rabbits
items.content: An ambitious plan to reduce rabbits and hares inside a DOC scientific reserve near Christchurch to protect rare native plants begins today with a targeted bait laying operation using helicopters.

items.title: Rāhui declared following death on Pouākai Range
items.content: Ka tipare a Taranaki ki te raukawakawa, he tohu aroha nui ki te iwi kua ngaro.

items.title: Allocation process for flight landings in Westland Tai Poutini National Park opens
items.content: Applications for the Westland Tai Poutini National Park aircraft landings allocation process have opened and will close 5 pm Friday 24 April 2026.

items.title: DOC and Meridian Energy celebrate 10-years of powering Kākāpō Recovery
items.content: DOC and Meridian Energy have renewed a longstanding partnership supporting the world-leading Kākāpō Recovery Programme for a further three years.

items.title: Call for proposals to restore the historic Chateau Tongariro
items.content: Proposals are being sought from experienced operators to restore and operate the historic Chateau Tongariro and its associated buildings, Conservation Minister Tama Potaka says.

items.title: Coromandel brown kiwi move off Motutapu for the first time
items.content: The first three kiwi were transferred back to the Hauraki-Coromandel region as part of a 10-day muster.

items.title: Strong summer of naturing wraps up as storm recovery continues
items.content: The 2025/26 summer visitor season has wrapped up, with more New Zealanders exploring a wider range of conservation areas despite storm disruptions.

items.title: Speargrass surprise revealed after Tongariro fires
items.content: The fire-charred ground at Tongariro has revealed some unexpected good news, with DOC ecologists discovering an abundance of a threatened plant species.

items.title: Dolphin necropsy inconclusive, not thought to be fishing related
items.content: Final necropsy results for a deceased Māui or Hector's dolphin that washed up on a remote Port Waikato coastline could not determine the cause of death.

items.title: Tech tool used to target pest plants at precious wetland
items.content: A high-tech tool has been used to control the invasive pest plant royal fern at Whangamarino.

items.title: Large kea flock ‘micro-chipped’ to track travels
items.content: An unusually large flock of young kea visiting Aoraki Mount Cook National Park has become part of a trial to track the movements of this far-flying parrot.

items.title: The egg race – hopeful signs for the Kākāriki Karaka recovery programme
items.content: For the first time, eggs from the critically endangered kākāriki karaka/orange-fronted parakeet have been retrieved from a Nelson predator free sanctuary and flown to Christchurch for hatching.

items.title: Waipoua Forest embarks on bold Kauri Walks expansion to enhance visitor experience and protect trees
items.content: The Kauri Walks tracks in Waipoua Forest are closed for naturing from March until the end of October 2026.

items.title: Tracks reopen as Pirongia storm damage clean-up continues
items.content: Several tracks in Pirongia Forest Park have reopened as DOC continues the clean-up across the maunga.

items.title: Old photos reveal big changes in West Coast marine environment
items.content: Newly discovered historic photos are shining a light onto dramatic changes in the West Coast marine environment.

items.title: Otago Hector’s dolphin project to reveal links to other populations
items.content: Field work for a project to better understand Hector’s dolphins in the Otago region will restart next week.

items.title: Beaked whale found dead after initial refloat
items.content: The Shepherd’s beaked whale that was refloated near St Heliers in Auckland on Tuesday 3 March was found deceased on the afternoon of Wednesday 4 March off Hobsonville.

items.title: Tongariro Alpine Crossing transport operator suspended
items.content: A company providing visitor transport to and from the Tongariro Alpine Crossing has had its DOC concession suspended for two weeks after ignoring a Hazardous Weather Warning.

items.title: Call for deer sightings as removal operation gets underway
items.content: Visitors to the southern parts of Coromandel Forest Park and landowners with adjacent properties are being urged to report deer sightings as an eradication operation gets underway.

items.title: Possible Māui dolphin found deceased
items.content: A deceased Māui or Hector’s dolphin found washed up on a remote Waikato coastline is being necropsied to try and learn more about threats to the species.

items.title: Pirongia storm damage assessments continue
items.content: Sections of some tracks across Pirongia Forest Park in Waikato may be closed for several weeks as DOC staff continue assessments for visitor safety and decide how any repairs will be managed.

items.title: Proposed changes aim to better target dolphin protection efforts
items.content: DOC is encouraging the public to have their say on a proposed amendment to the Te Pēwhairangi (Bay of Islands) Marine Mammal Sanctuary.

items.title: Motatapu track rerouted to avoid slow moving slip
items.content: A section of the Motatapu Track has been rerouted to avoid a large, slow-moving slip, DOC says.

items.title: Tauranga local captures photos of rare leatherback turtles
items.content: A Tauranga local has captured stunning close-up images of five rare leatherback turtles in the waters close to Mount Maunganui this week.

items.title: Vandals damage historic church
items.content: The only remaining building of a once-bustling historic gold mining town has been damaged in a disappointing display of vandalism.

items.title: Gecko repatriation closes curious trans-national case
items.content: Six jewelled geckos have been repatriated to New Zealand this month in a curious trans-national case involving the potential illegal smuggling of a rare and threatened taonga species.

items.title: Fire and Emergency New Zealand and DOC renew their Service Agreement
items.content: Fire and Emergency New Zealand and DOC have signed a new three-year Operational Service Agreement.

items.title: Report on under-pressure Waikato wetland delivers clear message
items.content: An assessment of the Whangamarino Wetland in north Waikato confirms decades of ecological decline and offers a clear roadmap for collaborative action to restore this internationally significant site.

items.title: New faces sought for Pureora Hunting Competition
items.content: Deer hunting veteran Allan Jackson is keen to see some new faces at this year's Pureora Hunting Competition prizegiving.

items.title: Muriwai gannets reveal flight paths for the first time
items.content: Researchers at DOC are tracking tākapu/Australasian gannets from the iconic Muriwai nesting colony to better understand their flight paths.

items.title: $3.5m boost to restore fire-damaged Tongariro
items.content: The Government is investing $3.5 million in the restoration of 3000-plus hectares of Tongariro National Park destroyed by two major fires last year, Conservation Minister Tama Potaka says.

items.title: Dolphin carrying dead calf seen in marine sanctuary
items.content: A bottlenose dolphin spotted carrying a dead calf on its back in the waters around Te Pēwhairangi/Bay of Islands needs the public to give it space.

items.title: New research on 1080 bait impact on Rakiura deer
items.content: Research from a predator control operation on Rakiura/Stewart Island to protect pukunui/southern New Zealand dotterel from extinction has provided valuable insights into the impact of 1080 bait on the island's non-target white tailed deer.

items.title: Celebration recognises philanthropic transformation of Abel Tasman National Park
items.content: An extraordinary contribution to New Zealand conservation was privately acknowledged this week in one of New Zealand's most iconic locations - the Abel Tasman National Park.

items.title: First kākāpō chick in four years hatches on Valentine's Day
items.content: The first kākāpō chick of the breeding season has hatched on Pukenui Anchor Island.

items.title: Temporary closure of Pirongia tracks following severe weather
items.content: DOC has temporarily closed several tracks on Pirongia following a significant weather event overnight on 13–14 February.

items.content: Musgravea track is currently closed due to extensive damage  and unsafe conditions caused by heavy rainfall earlier this year. Affected parks: Djiru National Park

items.title: Reduced capacity due to higher than expected water levels of Tinaroo Dam: Downfall Creek, Kauri Creek and Fong-On Bay camping areas, Danbulla National Park (update 7 Apr)
items.content: Higher than expected water levels of Tinaroo Dam have significantly reduced the amount of available space for camping at Downfall Creek, Kauri Creek and Fong-On Bay camping areas and space usually available for camping is currently underwater. Affected parks: Danbulla National Park and State Forest

items.title: Temporary closure: Bicton Hill walking track, Clump Mountain National Park (update 7 Apr)
items.content: Bicton Hill walking track is currently closed to the public due to a major landslip. Affected parks: Clump Mountain National Park

items.title: Severe weather (temporary full park closure) (update 2 Apr)
items.content: Due to the predicted severe weather and latest Tropical Cyclone Narelle  advice — the areas listed above are closed until declared safe. Affected parks: Flinders Group National Park (CYPAL); Turtle Group National Park; Lizard Island National Park

items.title: Temporary road closure: Bartle Frere access road (western trailhead), Wooroonooran National Park (update 1 Apr)
items.content: Due to recent severe weather impacts, the Bartle Frere access road at the western trailhead is closed. Affected parks: Wooroonooran National Park; Bartle Frere trail, Wooroonooran National Park

items.title: Planned aerial burning operations: Rungulla National Park, Canyon Resources Reserve and Undara Volcanic National Park (update)
items.content: Queensland Parks and Wildlife Service will be conducting aerial hazard reduction burns at Undara Volcanic National Park, Canyon Resource Reserve and Rungulla National Park from Tuesday 18 March to Friday 17 April 2026 (dates inclusive), pending weather conditions. Affected parks: Rungulla National Park; Canyon Resources Reserve; Undara Volcanic National Park

items.title: Wet season water safety information: Cardwell Forest Drive and Five Mile day-use area (update)
items.content: During the North Queensland wet season local heavy rainfall may cause significant stream rises at Attie Creek, Dead Horse Creek, the Spa Pool and Five Mile Creek. Affected parks: Cardwell State Forest

items.title: Wet season water safety information: Murray Falls, Girramay National Park (update)
items.content: During the North Queensland wet season local heavy rainfall may cause significant stream rises at Murray Falls, Girramay National Park. Affected parks: Girramay National Park

items.title: Temporary closures: southern section of Koolmoon Creek track, including Elizabeth Grant Falls; and part of Cannabullen Creek track, including Carter Creek and Cochable Creek camping areas—Misty Mountains wilderness tracks and Tully Gorge National Park (update 31 Mar)
items.content: Cassowary Coast Regional Council have closed the access road (H-Road) to Cochable Creek camping and day-use areas and the eastern entrance to Koolmoon Creek track, including Elizabeth Grant Falls section. The road closure is in place from its entrance point, off Tully Gorge Road and just before the Tully River bridge, due to safety concerns of a bridge structure along H-Road. Affected parks: Tully

items.title: Temporary closure of listed areas: Chillagoe-Mungana Caves National Park (update 31 Mar)
items.content: Due to recent wet weather and flooding, some facilities within Chillagoe-Mungana Caves National Park are temporarily closed until deemed safe to reopen. Anticipated reopening dates will be updated as conditions are assessed, please see below for further details. Affected parks: Chillagoe-Mungana Caves National Park

items.title: Emergency public phone out of order: Goldsborough Valley, Wooroonooran National Park
items.content: The emergency public phone at Goldsborough Valley camping area is currently out of order and will remain non-operational until further notice. Affected parks: Goldfield trail, Wooroonooran National Park; Wooroonooran National Park; Goldsborough Valley, Wooroonooran National Park

items.title: Safety advice: Track to Ussher Point severely eroded, Apudthama National Park (CYPAL)
items.content: Visitors are advised that due to heavy rainfall, the access road into Ussher Point is severely eroded. This includes the entire 55km section of track from the Bamaga Bypass Road to  Ussher Point camping area. Affected parks: Apudthama National Park (CYPAL)

items.title: Safety advice: Track to Captain Billy Landing severely eroded, Apudthama National Park (CYPAL)
items.content: Visitors are advised that due to heavy rainfall, the access road into Captain Billy Landing is severely eroded. This includes the entire 27km section of track from the Bamaga Bypass Road to Captain Billy Landing camping area. Affected parks: Apudthama National Park (CYPAL)

items.title: Temporary closure: Mima Teti camping area, Tagalaka National Park (update 27 Mar)
items.content: Due to extensive damage to the access road, caused by previous severe weather events, Mima Teti camping area is temporarily closed until it is deemed safe to reopen. Affected parks: Tagalaka National Park

items.title: Temporary wet season closure: Cardwell Range track, Cannabullen Creek camping area,  part of Cannabullen Creek track and Hinson Creek Camping area, Misty Mountains wilderness tracks (update 26 Mar)
items.content: Cardwell Range track, including Cannabullen Creek camping area,  and part of Cannabullen Creek track including Hinson Creek camping area, Misty Mountains wilderness tracks, are temporarily closed for the wet season. Affected parks: Wooroonooran National Park; Tully Falls National Park; Tablelands section, Tully Gorge National Park; Misty Mountains wilderness tracks

items.title: Temporary closure: Rungulla camping area, Rungulla National Park
items.content: Due to extensive damage to the access road caused by previous severe weather events, Rungulla camping area is temporarily closed until it is deemed safe to reopen. Affected parks: Rungulla National Park

items.title: Temporary closure: Keatings Lagoon  Conservation Park (update)
items.content: Due to flooding caused by Tropic Cyclone Narelle, Keatings Lagoon Conservation Park is closed to ensure visitor safety. Affected parks: Keatings Lagoon Conservation Park

items.title: Wet season closure: Palmer Goldfield Resources Reserve (update 23 Mar)
items.content: Palmer Goldfield Resources Reserve is closed for the wet season as roads into and on the park become impassable after heavy rain. Affected parks: Palmer Goldfield Resources Reserve

items.title: Wet season closure: Canyon Resources Reserve (update)
items.content: Canyon Resources Reserve is closed for the wet season. Affected parks: Canyon Resources Reserve

items.title: Safety advice: Eliot Falls access road is severely eroded, Apudthama National Park (CYPAL)
items.content: Visitors are advised that due to heavy rainfall, the access road into Eliot Falls is severely eroded. This includes the entire 8km section of track from the Bamaga Bypass Road to the Eliot Falls camping and day-use area. Affected parks: Apudthama National Park (CYPAL)

items.title: Planned burn for Lizard Island National Park
items.content: Queensland Parks and Wildlife Service (QPWS) will be conducting a planned burn/s (weather permitting) 10 May - 15 May 2026, as part of the fire management program for Lizard Island National Park Affected parks: Lizard Island National Park

items.title: BBQ temporarily out of service, Josephine Falls day-use area, Wooroonooran National Park (update 12 Mar)
items.content: The BBQ at Josephine Falls day-use area is currently out of service. Queensland Parks and Wildlife Service are currently working to fix the problem and apologise for any inconvenience. This alert will be removed when the BBQ has been fixed. Affected parks: Wooroonooran National Park; Josephine Falls, Wooroonooran National Park

items.title: No access to tap water: Goldsborough Valley, Wooroonooran National Park
items.content: Visitors are advised that there is currently no access to tap water in the following areas in Goldsborough Valley: Affected parks: Goldsborough Valley, Wooroonooran National Park; Wooroonooran National Park

items.title: Planned Burn For Flinders Group National Park (CYPAL)
items.content: Queensland Parks and Wildlife Service (QPWS) will be conducting a planned burn/s (weather permitting) from February 2026 to September 2026, as part of the fire management program for Flinders Group National Park. Affected parks: Flinders Group National Park (CYPAL)

items.title: Scheduled dewatering works affecting river levels: Kareeya power station, Tully River, Tully Gorge National Park
items.content: From Tuesday 4 August to Friday 5 September 2026 (dates inclusive), CleanCo will undertake dewatering of the Kareeya hydro-power station. Affected parks: Tablelands section, Tully Gorge National Park; Tully section, Tully Gorge National Park; Tully Gorge National Park

items.title: Temporary day-use area toilet facility closure: Goldsborough Valley, Wooroonooran National Park (update 4 Feb)
items.content: Due to public safety concerns surrounding exposed fibre asbestos,  the day-use area toilet facility will be closed until further notice. There is an alternative toilet facility located at the Bora Ground camping area. Affected parks: Wooroonooran National Park; Goldsborough Valley, Wooroonooran National Park

items.title: Planned works for Stephens Island Camping area
items.content: Queensland Parks and Wildlife Service (QPWS) will be conducting Restoration of Reef Islands work program at Stephens Island within the Barnard Island National Park from 15/02/2026 to 31/12/2026 (weather permitting). The result of this works means one camping node will be closed within these dates. Affected parks: Barnard Island Group National Park

items.title: Wet season closure: Gorrell track, Downey Creek camping area and Maple Creek Road, Misty Mountains wilderness tracks, Wooroonooran National Park (update)
items.content: The Gorrell track, including the Downey Creek camping area, and Maple Creek Road are closed for wet season. The track, camping area and road are anticipated to reopen Friday 29 May 2026, subject to access and weather conditions. Affected parks: Misty Mountains wilderness tracks; Palmerston (Doongan), Wooroonooran National Park; Wooroonooran National Park; Japoon National Park

items.title: Wet season closure: Lama Lama National Park (CYPAL) (update 19 Dec)
items.content: Lama Lama National Park (CYPAL) is closed for the wet season. Affected parks: Lama Lama National Park (CYPAL)

items.title: Safety advice: Scrubby Creek crossing into Eliot Falls camping and day-use area, Apudthama National Park (CYPAL) (update 5 Dec)
items.content: The entry into Eliot Falls camping and day-use area requires crossing Scrubby Creek on the Old Telegraph Track (managed by Cook Shire Council). This crossing is only suitable for high clearance four-wheel-drive vehicles due to severe erosion of the entry and exit points. Affected parks: Apudthama National Park (CYPAL)

items.title: Wet season closure: Cape Melville National Park (CYPAL) (update 1 Dec)
items.content: Cape Melville National Park (CYPAL) is closed for the wet season. The park is anticipated to reopen Saturday 1 August 2026, subject to road and weather conditions. Affected parks: Cape Melville National Park (CYPAL)

items.title: Temporary closure: ladies change shelter, Lake Eacham, Crater Lakes National Park (update 1 Dec)
items.content: The ladies change shelter at Lake Eacham day-use area is currently closed. Extensive damage to the floor structure has been detected and identified as a significant safety risk to users. Affected parks: Crater Lakes National Park

items.title: Wet season closure: Oyala Thumotang National Park (CYPAL) (update)
items.content: Oyala Thumotang National Park (CYPAL) is closed for the wet season. Affected parks: Oyala Thumotang National Park (CYPAL)

items.title: Wet season closure: Rinyirru (Lakefield) National Park (CYPAL) (update 1 Dec)
items.content: Rinyirru  (Lakefield) National Park (CYPAL) is closed for the wet season. Predicting re-opening dates is a challenge and will depend on type of wet season, road and weather conditions, as well as ensuring camp sites are safe to re-open. Affected parks: Rinyirru (Lakefield) National Park (CYPAL)

items.title: Temporary closure due to impacts from ex-Tropical Cyclone Jasper: Ngalba-bulal National Park (CYPAL) (update 28 Nov)
items.content: Ngalba-bulal National Park (CYPAL) is currently closed until further notice due to the impacts from ex-Tropical Cyclone Jasper. Affected parks: Ngalba-bulal National Park (CYPAL)

items.title: Wet season closure: Olkola National Park (CYPAL) (update)
items.content: Olkola National Park (CYPAL) is closed for the wet season.  Affected parks: Olkola National Park (CYPAL)

items.title: Wet season closure: Errk Oykangand National Park (CYPAL) (update 3 Nov)
items.content: Errk Oykangand National Park (CYPAL) is closed for the wet season. It is anticipated to reopen Wednesday 1 July 2026, subject to weather and road conditions. Affected parks: Errk Oykangand National Park (CYPAL)

items.title: George Point toilet removed due to erosion (update 28 Oct)
items.content: George Point camping area on the southern coastline of Hinchinbrook Island National Park has been affected by severe erosion due to ongoing high tides over the past several years. Affected parks: Thorsborne Trail, Hinchinbrook Island National Park; Hinchinbrook Island National Park

items.content: As there are numerous parks in the Cape York Peninsula area, park alerts are published on individual park pages. Affected parks: Cape York Peninsula parks

items.title: No access to Saad Point: adjacent to Apudthama National Park (CYPAL) (update 25 Jul)
items.content: Visitors are advised that due to safety and road condition concerns and until further notice, there is no access to Saad Point. This area is normally accessed via the Ussher Point track in Apudthama National Park (CYPAL). Affected parks: Apudthama National Park (CYPAL)

items.title: Upcoming temporary camping area closure for planned burn: Ussher Point camping area, Apudthama National Park (CYPAL)
items.content: Visitors are advised that Queensland Parks and Wildlife Service will be conducting planned burns at Ussher Point camping area from Monday 15 June to Friday 19 June 2026 (dates inclusive). Affected parks: Apudthama National Park (CYPAL)

items.title: Temporary closure: camp site 4 Ussher Point camping area, Apudthama National Park (CYPAL) (update 20 Jun)
items.content: Camp site 4 at Ussher Point camping area will be closed for safety reasons until further notice. Affected parks: Apudthama National Park (CYPAL)

items.content: Due to  heavy rain from ex-Tropical Cyclone Jasper Mount Edith Road, Kauri Creek Road and Tinaroo Creek Road are closed until further notice. Affected parks: Danbulla National Park and State Forest

items.title: In Liguria c’è un borgo tra mare e vette che ha ispirato i rapper più famosi: ecco qual è
items.content: Orientarsi a Cogoleto: mare, storia e caruggi Cogoleto si trova sulla Riviera di Ponente, tra Arenzano e Varazze, lungo quel tratto di Liguria dove la ferrovia corre quasi a filo dell’acqua. Il centro è raccolto, con caruggi stretti, case alte e persiane colo…

items.title: Il borgo lombardo sul Lago Maggiore dove il tramonto toglie il fiato e si sale in cima sospesi nel vuoto
items.content: Dove si trova Laveno-Mombello e perché sceglierla Laveno-Mombello si affaccia sulla sponda lombarda del Lago Maggiore, tra Leggiuno e Porto Valtravaglia, in una grande insenatura naturale. La sua posizione è particolare: davanti c’è la costa piemontese con Ve…
